title: Urvil’s six-hitting carnage: 200-6s-a-day, Dhoni’s special bat and a promise
content: Urvil Patel’s explosive 65 off 23 balls against Lucknow Super Giants announced the arrival of CSK’s newest six-hitting sensation. The wicketkeeper-batter smashed six sixes within his first eight balls, rewrote IPL records. Backed by advice, and even a specially weighted bat from MS Dhoni, Urvil’s power-hitting philosophy is shaped by a gruelling routine of 200 practice sixes a day.

title: Google's top scientist to EU: Our Red Team 'hacked' in less than 2 hours
content: Google's top scientist Sergei Vassilvitskii is reportedly furious with the EU's proposal to share search data with rivals, warning it risks exposing private user information.  He claims their 'red team' can break the EU's anonymization scheme in under two hours, raising serious privacy and security concerns.  Regulators will decide by July 27.

title: Kerala CM suspense continues as Congress brass weighs claims; contenders maintain silence
content: KPCC president Sunny Joseph stated that AICC president Mallikarjun Kharge and Rahul Gandhi will soon announce Kerala's next chief minister after interviewing three contenders. Joseph indicated no further state-level discussions are needed, with the Congress leadership holding the final decision. Camps backing K.C. Venugopal, V.D. Satheesan, and Ramesh Chennithala remain hopeful, each presenting their case to the high command.

title: JEE Advanced admit cards out: Direct link to download hall ticket here
content: The Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Roorkee has released the JEE Advanced 2026 admit cards on the official portal. Registered candidates can now download their hall tickets using their login credentials. The entrance examination will be conducted on May 18 in two shifts, and carrying the admit card along with a valid photo ID is mandatory for entry.

title: IPL 2026 playoff scenarios: MI, LSG knocked out; RCB’s chances soar to 94.82%
content: Royal Challengers Bengaluru became IPL 2026’s strongest playoff favourites after their last-ball win over Mumbai Indians, reaching a 94.82% qualification probability and 63.41% chance of a top-two finish. SRH, GT and PBKS also remain strong contenders, while CSK revived their campaign. Rajasthan Royals and KKR still have hope, but Delhi Capitals face slim chances. Mumbai Indians and Lucknow Super Giants are officially out of playoff contention.

title: Daily wage earners’ suicide 31% of total, highest in a decade
content: Daily wage earners constituted 31% of all suicide deaths in 2024, the highest in a decade, with 52,910 such fatalities. This marks a significant increase from previous years, as the total number of suicides nationwide rose to 1.7 lakh. Family problems and illness remain the primary reported causes for these tragic deaths.

title: Mother’s Day horror in UP: Man guns down mom, brother over property dispute
content: A man allegedly shot and killed his mother and younger brother in Banda following a dispute over ancestral property. The incident occurred after an altercation regarding the division of land, during which other family members were also injured. The accused fled the scene and police are actively searching for him.

title: American CEO: US immigration needs weekly H-1B dashboard as distortion fuels anti-Indian sentiment
content: American CEO James Blunt urges USCIS for a transparent H-1B visa dashboard, citing public misunderstanding. He argues that many approvals are renewals, not new workers, fueling misinformation. Blunt's call for data on net changes, extensions, and transfers aims to clarify the system and counter anti-immigrant sentiment.

title: SAI suspends boxing trials, throws CWG and Asian Games selection into chaos
content: Indian boxing faces administrative chaos as the Sports Authority of India (SAI) halts selection trials for upcoming Commonwealth and Asian Games. A dispute over transparency in evaluation criteria, selection committees, and coach appointments has stalled preparations.  Boxers who excelled in the unrecognised COAS Cup are now seeking legal recourse for a fair chance.

title: Did govt block Rahul Gandhi's Instagram reel with Vijay? What ministry said
content: The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology has denied blocking Rahul Gandhi's social media posts featuring Tamil Nadu Chief Minister Vijay. Sources stated that Instagram's internal system mistakenly flagged the content, which has since been restored. This clarification comes after Congress alleged the ministry was suppressing opposition voices.

title: All caste team: Yogi Adityanath inducts 3 OBCs, 2 Dalits, 1 Brahmin as UP ministers
content: In a strategic move ahead of upcoming polls, the BJP expanded its Yogi cabinet, inducting six new ministers. The reshuffle heavily favors backward and Dalit communities, aiming to counter the Samajwadi Party's 'PDA' narrative. This expansion, the second and likely last, brings the ministry to its maximum strength, balancing caste and regional representation.

title: After 43 yrs, US family sells electrical firm for $1.7bn; gives 540 workers $240m
content: A US-based businessman, Graham Walker, gifted his 540 employees a staggering $240 million from the $1.7 billion sale of his family's company, Fibrebond Corp. This generous act, where workers received an average of $443,000, stemmed from a condition Walker imposed before the sale to power-management giant Eaton.

title: 'Can't keep a good man down': Krunal gets emotional - Watch
content: Royal Challengers Bengaluru beat Mumbai Indians by two wickets in a last-ball IPL 2026 thriller in Raipur. Krunal Pandya starred with a gritty 73 despite severe cramps, rescuing RCB from 39/3 with key partnerships. Bhuvneshwar Kumar finished the chase with a crucial six. After the match, an emotional Krunal praised former MI teammate Kieron Pollard, calling him his “big brother".

title: Non-binary Indian on student visa elected to Scottish Parliament
content: A non-binary transgender Indian national, Q Manivannan, has been elected as a Member of the Scottish Parliament (MSP) on a student visa. This historic win marks the first time a foreign citizen without permanent residency has held such a position. Manivannan's election has drawn controversy, with debates arising over their immigration status and political stance.

title: Tollywood actor Bharat Kanth, cameraman killed as car rams into truck in Telangana
content: A tragic road accident on Hyderabad's Outer Ring Road claimed the lives of Tollywood actor Bharat Kanth and cameraman Trilok. Their car collided with a truck from behind in the early hours of Sunday, resulting in their immediate deaths. The duo was returning to Hyderabad from Nellore when the incident occurred.

title: 'Healthy, asymptomatic': 2 Indians from Hantavirus-hit ship shifted to quarantine in Netherlands
content: Two Indian crew members aboard the Dutch cruise ship MV Hondius, which anchored off Spain due to a hantavirus scare, are confirmed healthy and asymptomatic. Evacuated to the Netherlands for quarantine, their well-being is being closely monitored by Indian authorities in coordination with Spanish officials.  This incident highlights global health vigilance.

title: PM Modi pushes for cut in fuel use & gold buying, favours work from home
content: Amid global energy disruptions, PM Modi urged Indians to conserve foreign exchange by reducing reliance on imported fuel. He advocated for public transport, work-from-home, avoiding overseas trips and non-essential gold purchases for a year. The Prime Minister also encouraged farmers to halve chemical fertilizer use and shift towards natural farming.

title: PNB, Marico & more: Top stocks to watch on May 11 — check list
content: Kotak Securities maintains an add rating on PNB with a revised target of Rs 125, citing steady profitability despite weaker core earnings than peers. Jefferies reiterates a buy on Marico, raising its target to Rs 960, with management confident in strong EBITDA growth.

title: Oil prices jump after Trump rejects Iran’s latest offer; Hormuz remains shut
content: Oil prices surged as President Trump rejected Iran's peace proposal, reigniting fears over global energy supply routes. Hopes for breakthrough talks faded, shifting market attention to Trump's upcoming visit to Beijing. Saudi Aramco warned of significant oil market losses, while tankers navigated the Strait of Hormuz with tracking systems off.

title: Kohli spotted glued to screen during Urvil’s explosive 13-ball fifty - Watch
content: Virat Kohli watched Urvil Patel's explosive innings for Chennai Super Kings. Patel hit the joint-fastest IPL fifty in just 13 balls. He smashed six sixes in his first eight balls, a new IPL record. Patel scored 65 off 23 balls. Chennai Super Kings won the match by five wickets. Lucknow Super Giants had earlier scored 203/8.

title: ‘Totally unacceptable’: Trump rejects Iran’s ceasefire response sent via Pakistan
content: Iran has responded to a US ceasefire proposal through Pakistani mediators, seeking a permanent end to regional conflicts and guarantees for maritime security. President Trump rejected Tehran's position, calling it "TOTALLY UNACCEPTABLE!" amid rising tensions and drone incidents in the Gulf.

title: Accountsmaxxing? Decoding Gen Z's chaotic yet genius approach to finance
content: Gen Z is navigating a complex financial landscape, balancing early earning and investing with impulsive spending and trend-driven bets. While digitally savvy and embracing new tools, they often overlook essential safety nets like insurance. Experts urge a shift from quick gains to disciplined, long-term wealth building, splitting finances strategically for stability.

title: Trump to visit China on May 13: Trade, Taiwan and more — what’s on the table
content: President Donald Trump is set to visit China from May 13-15 for talks with President Xi Jinping, focusing on easing trade tensions and discussing Iran. Broader issues like tariffs and Taiwan are also on the agenda. The visit aims to rebalance the relationship, prioritizing reciprocity and fairness for American economic independence.

title: Himanta to take oath as Assam CM tomorrow
content: Himanta Biswa Sarma has been appointed Assam's chief minister by governor Lakshman Prasad Acharya, marking the NDA's third consecutive term. Following his unanimous election as leader of the BJP-led NDA legislature party, Sarma staked his claim with support from 102 MLAs. He will be sworn in on May 12, with Prime Minister Modi expected to attend.

title: India, UAE working on pact to help in evacuation of Indians via Fujairah port
content: India and UAE are finalizing an agreement to facilitate the evacuation of Indian workers via Fujairah port, a move likely to be signed during PM Modi's upcoming stopover. This pact addresses potential disruptions from the West Asia conflict, offering a crucial maritime route for over 4 million Indian workers in the UAE.

title: Why Raj Angad Bawa bowled the last over: SKY explains bold call vs RCB
content: Royal Challengers Bengaluru secured a dramatic two-wicket victory over Mumbai Indians on the final ball, eliminating MI from the tournament. Despite a late surge, MI's captain's final over gamble didn't pay off. RCB's win propels them to the top of the IPL 2026 points table, boosting their playoff aspirations.

title: Plane carrying 22 UK citizens evacuated from hantavirus-hit ship lands in Manchester
content: Evacuees from the hantavirus-hit MV Hondius cruise ship have landed in the Netherlands, with passengers disembarking in good spirits. Authorities have begun quarantine procedures for the 26 individuals, including eight Dutch nationals, who arrived on a flight from Tenerife.  The ship experienced a hantavirus outbreak, leading to three passenger deaths.

title: NTPC to approach Centre with feasibility report for its first N-power plant
content: NTPC is preparing to submit a feasibility report to the Centre for its first nuclear energy plant, aiming to contribute to India's goal of 100 GW atomic power capacity by 2047. The company has identified land in Bihar and plans to set up 30 GW of nuclear capacity across 14 states by 2047, with initial units of 700 MW each.

title: After poll win, BJP’s fish fest to reassure voters
content: Following their swearing-in, BJP MLAs Agnimitra Paul, Dilip Ghosh, and Tapas Roy organized a 'fish festival' across Kolkata to counter perceptions of the party's aversion to non-vegetarian food. The events, attended by numerous party workers and locals, featured traditional 'machh-bhat' lunches, aiming to reassure voters about preserving Bengali culinary identity.

title: Suvendu Adhikari may move into new home, just 2km from Didi’s
content: Bengal's new Chief Minister, Suvendu Adhikari, is considering relocating his Kolkata residence to the underutilized Soujanya complex in Alipore. Police have assessed the security at the palatial building, constructed for VVIPs. Adhikari, who has emphasized accessibility at his ancestral home, may opt for the more centralized Soujanya due to security needs.

title: Asim Munir calls 2025 conflict 'battle of two ideologies'
content: Following India's strong warning, Pakistan's Army Chief declared the recent conflict a battle of ideologies, claiming victory. He asserted Pakistan's strategy was superior, framing the outcome as truth triumphing over falsehood. Meanwhile, a Pakistani minister's statement highlighting the Prime Minister and Army Chief's leadership drew public criticism, underscoring the military's significant influence.

title: Observatory that put Mount Everest in record books now lies in ruins
content: Bhuri Tori, a forgotten scientific outpost near Sironj, once served as the geodetic heart of undivided India. British surveyors used this ridge for the Great Trigonometrical Survey, fixing coordinates and calculating distances that helped measure Mount Everest. Today, this historically significant site lies neglected, with its structures vandalized and stones stolen.

title: Boeing Honcho front-runner for NSIL chief post, could be first outsider
content: India's space commercial arm, NSIL, is poised for a significant shift as a private sector leader, reportedly from Boeing, is the front-runner for its CMD post. This marks a departure from its history of being led by Isro insiders. The selection process is in its final stages, aiming to bring external expertise to the burgeoning space PSU.

title: Tradition of singing Tamil Thaai Vazhthu at beginning, National Anthem at end will continue, says Minister Aadhav Arjuna
content: In Tamil Nadu, the rendering of Tamil Thai Vazhthu as the third song is not acceptable to the Tamil Nadu Government led by the TVK, he said

title: Kerala Government formation LIVE: Impasse over chief ministerial candidate in Congress
content: Congress president Mallikarjun Kharge and Leader of Opposition in Lok Sabha Rahul held consultations with three main contenders on May 9, 2026

title: 1998 Pokhran nuclear tests reflected India's scientific excellence: PM on National Technology Day
content: India conducted five nuclear tests of advanced weapon designs on May 11 and 13 in 1998 at the Pokhran range in Rajasthan desert

title: Auto drivers seek hike in minimum fare amid rising LPG prices in Bengaluru
content: At present, the minimum auto meter fare in Bengaluru is ₹36, while passengers are charged ₹18 for every additional kilometre. The associations are now demanding that the minimum fare be increased to ₹50 and the subsequent fare to ₹25 per kilometre.

title: Liquor manufacturers seek hike in prices citing West Asia crisis
content: Glass bottles, the primary packaging format for spirits, have been severely impacted owing to the energy-intensive nature of glass manufacturing; prices of plastic caps have gone up

title: Five killed, including two children, in Mahbubnagar bypass road accident
content: The accident occurred when a sports bike, a Suzuki Hayabusa (HR 26 BP 1491) rammed into a car trying to take turn at the X-road between 12.30 a.m. and 1 a.m.

title: Two Nellore residents killed in car crash on ORR near Adibatla
content: Police said the two were travelling in a car along the stretch between Bongloor and Thukkuguda when the accident occurred near exit number 12

title: Belagavi Inspector-General of Police completes Sydney Ironman
content: Competing in the M45-49 category, Mr. Patil completed the gruelling event — featuring a 1.9 km swim, 90 km cycling and 21.1 km half marathon — in seven hours 16 minutes and 25 seconds

title: Belagavi man is all-India topper in Indian Forest Service examination
content: Basavaraj Dhareppa Kempawad, from Athani in Belagavi, has secured the all-India first rank in the Indian Forest Service examination

title: MP boat tragedy: Government forms judicial probe panel; to submit report in three months
content: will probe the causes of the accident, examine whether adequate rescue and relief measures were taken, and fix responsibility, says the official

title: Prevalence of fake currency still a reality post-demonetisation
content: Gujarat accounted for more than half the value of counterfeit currency seizures between 2017 and 2024

title: Increasing strength of Parliament to 850 a ‘joke’, says Tharoor
content: BJP leader K. Annamalai said the delimitation exercise was a “grand bargain” between the North and South Indian States before the outcome of the ongoing Census is made public

title: The emerging Rahul Gandhi-Vijay political partnership
content: If the TVK government was to face a challenge from the Union government, TVK president C. Joseph Vijay could find support from Congress

title: Congress’ wait to join the Tamil Nadu Cabinet continues
content: Party sources said the Congress asked the TVK to accommodate Melur MLA P. Viswanathan and Congress Legislature Party leader S. Rajeshkumar in the Cabinet

title: CM selection a democratic process: Chennithala
content: The senior Congress leader slams BJP, says the grand old party does not function on instructions from Nagpur

title: Two Indians aboard cruise ship with hantavirus cases evacuated to Netherlands
content: Indian Ambassador to Spain Jayant N. Khobragade is in close contact with Spanish authorities and the two Indians to assure their well-being and safety

title: Commercial flight operations come to a halt at Gondia airport
content: According to Airport Director Girishchandra Verma, IndiGo and Star Air have stopped operating services from Gondia due to “technical and operational” reasons

title: Crumbling of Awadh’s heritage amidst monument encroachments
content: A recent CAG report highlights extensive encroachments on monuments in Uttar Pradesh, raising concerns among heritage conservationists that the syncretic legacy of Awadh could be lost without urgent action

title: Biogas association calls for blending of organic manure with fertilizers
content: 10% mandatory blending of fermented organic manure could save $2 billion in imports annually, and improve soil fertility, says Indian Biogas Association

title: Suvendu Adhikari to chair first Cabinet meeting on May 11
content: On Monday, Suvendu Adhikari will enter Nabanna for the first time as the Chief Minister; at his first cabinet meeting, portfolios will be allocated to five Ministers

title: Dravidian parties got trapped in vicious cycle of cash-for-votes and corruption, says T.N. Minister Arunraj
content: “The Dravidian parties played a role in making T.N. a social justice State. But their initial idealism became diluted. They got trapped in a narrow path of corruption-ridden governance and family politics to such an extent that they couldn’t come out of it,” says the TVK leader

title: PM visit turns city into high security zone as crowds throng Parade Grounds
content: Tight security, massive mobilisation and political spectacle mark Modi’s city visit for multiple programmes, culminating in a public meeting at Parade Grounds attended by over 75,000 people

title: Maritime safety and security is of primordial importance to Indian Ocean Region: Secretary-General
content: Any disruption in the Indian Ocean will have a deep impact on the economy across the region, says Sanjiv Ranjan, Secretary-General of the Indian Ocean Rim Association; war in West Asia overshadows India’s agenda for IORA summit next year, marking 30 years of the grouping

title: British-era Lakshmanaswami Town Hall restored in Vellore
content: The government hall once housed the district museum of North Arcot region when it was established in 1985 by the Department of Education

title: No intent to insult, says Apu Joseph amid KC(M) protest
content: Kerala Congress leader and MLA-elect says his statement about welcoming Kerala Congress (M) with open arms was driven by a genuine wish that K.M. Mani’s party should not fade away

title: In Kerala, nearly half of Congress’ MLAs-elect are young faces
content: Congress decision to field maximum number of youth and student activists brought rich dividends as the young candidates connected well with Gen Z and young voters and won the popular mandate, feels one MLA-elect

title: CPI Kerala State secretary Binoy Viswam criticises personality cults, reiterates CPI claim to Deputy Opposition Leader’s post
content: After the CPI national council meeting in New Delhi, Viswam describes the “unexpected” LDF defeat as a warning issued by the people

title: Congress claims Instagram blocked Rahul Gandhi's reel and photo post featuring Vijay due to MeitY rules
content: Sources in the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology clarified that it had nothing to do with the action and it was because the platform’s internal system mistakenly flagged the post for blocking

title: AAP holds protests against BJP over ED action against Punjab Minister Sanjeev Arora
content: The AAP leaders and volunteers burnt effigies of the BJP-led Centre and raised slogans against it. They were also carrying placards which read 'ED-BJP gathjod murdabad'

title: Subsidy for power supply to domestic consumers to cross ₹10,000-crore mark
content: The TVK government’s announcement of 200 units of free power for domestic consumers using below 500 units bimonthly will further increase the State’s annual subsidy burden by nearly ₹2,000 crore

title: ED gets seven-day custody of Punjab Minister Sanjeev Arora
content: Mr. Arora was arrested on May 9 at his official residence in Chandigarh by the Enforcement Directorate in an alleged money-laundering case.

title: Move to secure technical sanction for STP in West Hill sparks protest
content: District Collector issues the direction at a recent meeting attended by officials from the Kozhikode Corporation, District Suchitwa Mission, State Pollution Control Board, and Local Self-Government department to initiate steps to obtain technical sanction for the project

title: India-Trinidad archival pact to help diaspora trace ancestral roots: Jaishankar
content: Mr. Jaishankar said Prime Minister Narendra Modi attached high importance to creating a database of the Girmitya community and conducting research on its legacy

title: Governor’s role in government formation | Explained
content: What does Article 164(1) say about the appointment of the Chief Minister? When does the Governor use discretionary powers? Which commissions recommended guidelines for government formation in a hung Assembly? Why has the conduct of Governors in hung Assemblies raised concerns?

title: Man held on charge of attacking another with hammer in Pudukad in Thrissur
content: Cyril of  Potta was taken into custody for attacking Deenas Davis, a native of Marathakkara; victim critical after head injuries

title: Tripura pharmacists to join strike on May 20
content: The All India Organisation of Chemists and Druggists has three major demands — stop online sale of medicines; stop deep discounts from corporate-run industry; and stop the sale of spurious medicines

title: PIL in SC for reviewing wages of priests, sevadars and temple staff in govt.-controlled temples
content: “Priests and temple staff are not getting even the minimum wage prescribed by the State for unskilled and semi-skilled workers. This is a systemic exploitation,” the plea said.

title: How 'Laughing Buddha' In Pokhran Permanently Altered India's Place In Global Politics
content: On Pokharan anniversary, we look at how India's techno diplomacy saved it from meeting the same fate as what is happening to Iran right now.

title: Jailed Iranian Nobel Laureate Gets Bail, Moved To Hospital In Tehran
content: Iran's imprisoned Nobel Peace Prize winner Narges Mohammadi has been moved to a hospital in the capital, Tehran, and has been granted a suspension of her sentence on heavy

title: Vijay's Power-Dressing Move During Oath, Ditches Traditional Shirt-Vesti
content: The well-tailored black suit paired with a white shirt gave Vijay a refined and formal appearance, but broke the decades of Tamil Nadu political tradition.

title: US-Iran War News LIVE Updates: "Blow'em Up": Trump Warns Iran Against Moving Uranium, Says US Keeping An Eye
content: US-Iran Ceasefire Live Updates: A US admiral said US forces sank six small Iranian ships. Iran denied any had been sunk and earlier fired warning shots at US warships

title: AI Courses Are India's New Craze. But Are They Helping Students Land Jobs?
content: National Technology Day: From campuses to workplaces, Indians are racing to learn AI for jobs, fuelling a nationwide surge in practical AI courses.

title: Video: Turkish Airlines Plane Tyre Catches Fire During Landing In Nepal
content: Turkish Airlines aircraft caught fire on right tyres during landing at Tribhuvan Airport, passengers evacuated safely.

title: Israel Gets $3.8 Billion US Military Aid A Year. Netanyahu Wants It To Be 0
content: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu hopes to wean Israel off U.S. military support within a decade as his country pushes to strengthen ties with Gulf states, he said in an

title: Vijay's 'Star-Style' Oath As Chief Minister Marks New Era In Tamil Nadu
content: For many, it was not merely a swearing-in ceremony but the arrival of a completely different political culture in Tamil Nadu.

title: "Totally Unacceptable": Trump Rejects Iran's Peace Plan, Tehran Responds
content: Iran responded to the US by warning it would not hold back from retaliating against any new US strikes or permit more foreign warships in the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Somnath Temple To See First-Ever Spire Kumbhabhishek. What It Means
content: For the first time in its modern history, a sacred Kumbhabhishek will be conducted atop the temple's 90-meter-high Shikhar using holy water gathered from 11 prominent pilgrimage sites across the country.

title: Donald Trump Accepts Xi's Invitation, To Visit China From May 13-15
content: President Donald Trump will visit China from May 13 to 15, Beijing confirmed on Monday, with the US leader expected to discuss Iran and...

title: Mother's Day Gift Ideas: Five Financial Plans To Give Her Lifelong Security
content: Mother's Day Gift Ideas: Financial planners and real estate experts say the Mother's Day gift need not be symbolic. It could be structural.

title: No Pink Saheli Card, No Free Bus Ride? Delhi Eyes Mandatory Use From July
content: The move is part of a broader plan to integrate the scheme with the National Common Mobility Card (NCMC) platform under the Centre's "One Nation, One Card" initiative.

title: Vijay Oath Ceremony Highlights: "I'm Like Your Son, Your Brother," Vijay Tells Supporters After Taking Oath
content: Vijay Oath Ceremony LIVE Updates: Vijay, the Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK) chief, now has the support of 120 MLAs.

title: Bangladesh can be a referee between India, China, but not football: Tarique aide
content: foreign affairs adviser: Bangladesh will not be a 'football' between India and China . comments come as dhaka looks at balancing bilateral relations with several nations . he said: 'we can act as a referee from time to time to ensure fair play'

title: I'll be the only power centre: TVK chief Vijay after taking oath as Tamil Nadu CM
content: Ram M Sundaram is an Assistant Editor at The Times of India, Chennai, where he covers commute, trial courts, and political affairs.

title: Niti report says school access up, continuity weak
content: Manash Pratim Gohain is a seasoned journalist with over two decades at The Times of India, where he has built a rich body of work spanning education policy, politics, and governance. Renowned for his incisive coverage of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020, accreditation reforms, and skilling initiatives, he has also reported on student politics, urban policy, and social movements. His political reportage—both reflective and news-driven—adds depth to his writing, bridging policy with public impact. Through his 2,500 articles and related outlets, he has emerged as a trusted voice in national discourse, particularly in linking education reform to broader societal change. Read More

title: DMK stood by Congress for years only to be backstabbed: PM Modi
content: Pearl D’Souza is a principal correspondent at The Times of India, having dedicated nine years to journalism. She covers health, along with a wide range of other topics. She is particularly focused on the rapidly evolving health sector, with special emphasis on public health infrastructure. Her reporting spans education, science, and technology, food. She was certified as a Climate Correspondent by the Fulbright-Hays Public Diplomacy Program, organized by the US Mission India in 2024, and is a 2025 candidate for United Nation's RAF memorial journalism fellowship. title: After big wins, NDA sitting comfortably for 2027 presidential election
content: BJP's super-majority win in a string of assembly polls capped by its maiden victory in west Bengal with over two-thirds seats . its rising number in Rajya Sabha will all but allow the party to dictate terms in next year's presidential election . it will be effective in neutralising the loss it has suffered in LS where its tally fell from 303 to 240 in 2024 .

title: From going off script to big appointments & song row: Vijay's 1st day as Tamil Nadu CM
content: . The TOI News Desk comprises a dedicated and tireless team of journalists who operate around the clock to deliver the most current and comprehensive news and updates to the readers of The Times of India worldwide. With an unwavering commitment to excellence in journalism, our team is at the forefront of gathering, verifying, and presenting breaking news, in-depth analysis, and insightful reports on a wide range of topics. The TOI News Desk is your trusted source for staying informed and connected to the ever-evolving global landscape, ensuring that our readers are equipped with the latest developments that matter most."
